_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 11: Technical Documentation
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance, industry best practices, and relevant ISO standards. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 11 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: EU AI Act Annex IV (technical documentation requirements)
EU AI Act Recital 68 (legislative intent on documentation)
ISO/IEC TR 24028:2020 — Overview of trustworthiness in AI
ISO/IEC 23894:2023 — Guidance on risk management for AI
ISO/IEC TR 24029-1:2021 — Assessment of robustness of neural networks
Google Model Cards for Model Reporting
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 11 / Annex IV (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: System Description (Annex IV, Section 1)
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST maintain a README or equivalent document providing a general description of the AI system.
requirements.rationale: Annex IV(1)(a) requires a 'general description of the AI system' including intended purpose, provider identity, version, and how it interacts with hardware and software.
required_content: Versions of relevant software or firmware
Forms of market placement (SaaS, on-premise, embedded)
System description in plain language for non-technical users

requirements.title: Architecture Documentation (Annex IV, Section 2)
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST maintain architecture documentation describing system elements and the development process.
requirements.rationale: Annex IV(2)(a) requires 'a description of the elements of the AI system and of the process for its development, including design specifications.'
required_content: Key design choices and their rationale

requirements.title: API / Interface Documentation (Annex IV, Section 2(e))
requirements.requirement: The provider SHOULD maintain an API specification (e.g., OpenAPI/Swagger) describing input data, output data, and their specifications.
requirements.rationale: Annex IV(2)(e) requires a description of 'input data' and 'a description of the output of the system.' An API specification is the industry standard way to document this.

requirements.title: Model Card (Annex IV, Section 2(b-d))
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST maintain a model card documenting design specifications, model architecture, training methodologies, and key design choices.
requirements.rationale: Annex IV(2)(b-d) requires documentation of design specifications, computational resources, training methodologies, training data information, and key design choices.
required_content: Evaluation metrics and performance on benchmarks

requirements.title: Testing Documentation (Annex IV, Section 4)
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST maintain testing documentation describing validation and testing procedures and their outcomes.
requirements.rationale: Annex IV(4) requires documentation of 'validation and testing procedures used, including information about the validation and testing data used and their main characteristics, metrics used to measure accuracy, robustness and compliance with other relevant requirements, test logs, and all test reports.'

requirements.title: Change Log / Version History
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST maintain a change log documenting all significant modifications to the system.
requirements.rationale: Art. 11(1) requires documentation to be 'kept up to date.' A change log is the standard mechanism for tracking documentation currency.

requirements.title: Deployment Documentation
requirements.requirement: The provider SHOULD maintain deployment documentation describing how the system is deployed and operated.
requirements.rationale: Annex IV(1)(a) requires description of 'forms in which the AI system is placed on the market or put into service.' Deployment documentation ensures this information is available.
acceptable_implementations: CI/CD pipeline configuration with deployment steps

scoring.compliant: R01 + R02 + R04 + R05 met, and at least 2 of R03, R06, R07 met
scoring.partial: R01 met + at least 3 others met
scoring.non_compliant: R01 not met, or fewer than 3 requirements met
